incidents.parsed_error: AWS IAM permission denied: Action 's3:PutObject' is not authorized on resource 'arn:aws:s3:::prod-assets-bucket'. The GitHub Actions runner role 'github-deploy-role' is missing write access to this S3 bucket. This caused the 'Deploy Frontend Assets' step to fail at 02:14 UTC.

options.title: Attach S3 write policy to runner role
options.description: Modifies the existing 'github-deploy-role' IAM policy to explicitly allow s3:PutObject on the prod-assets-bucket. Lowest risk — scoped to this bucket only.

options.title: Create a new scoped S3 deployment policy
options.description: Creates a separate managed policy with least-privilege S3 access and attaches it to the runner role. Cleaner architecture, slightly more work.

options.title: Hold for manual resolution
options.description: Pause this incident and handle it manually. No automated action will be taken.

incidents.parsed_error: Kubernetes pod 'payment-service-7d9f8b-xkq2p' in namespace 'production' is in CrashLoopBackOff state. Container exit code 137 (OOMKilled) — the pod exceeded its 512Mi memory limit 4 times in the last 10 minutes. The payment-service deployment has been degraded since 01:52 UTC.

options.title: Increase memory limit to 1Gi
options.description: Updates the payment-service Deployment manifest to set resources.limits.memory: 1Gi. Generates a PR to your GitOps repo for approval before applying.

options.title: Add horizontal pod autoscaler
options.description: Configures HPA to scale payment-service between 2-10 replicas based on memory pressure, distributing load instead of increasing per-pod limits.

options.title: Roll back to previous deployment
options.description: Runs kubectl rollout undo deployment/payment-service to restore the last stable version while the memory issue is investigated.

options.title: Hold for manual resolution
options.description: Pause this incident and handle it manually.

incidents.parsed_error: Terraform drift detected on 3 resources in 'prod-us-east-1' workspace. Someone manually modified the 'api-server-sg' security group in the AWS console at 00:31 UTC — added inbound rule TCP:5432 from 0.0.0.0/0 (open PostgreSQL to the internet). This is a critical security misconfiguration. The Terraform state no longer matches live infrastructure.

options.title: Generate Terraform code to close the port
options.description: Agent writes the corrected aws_security_group_rule resource block and opens a PR to bring the Terraform config back in sync. The open 5432 rule will be removed on next apply.

options.title: Immediately close the port via AWS CLI
options.description: Runs aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress to close the 0.0.0.0/0:5432 rule NOW, then generates a PR to document the fix in Terraform.

options.title: Hold for manual resolution
options.description: Pause this incident and handle it manually.

incidents.parsed_error: Node.js dependency conflict in CI pipeline: 'react-scripts@5.0.1' requires 'webpack@4.x' but 'next@14.2.0' requires 'webpack@5.x'. Both are present in package-lock.json causing the build to fail at the 'npm ci' step with ERESOLVE error.

options.title: Remove react-scripts, consolidate on Next.js webpack
options.description: Removes react-scripts from package.json (redundant with Next.js) and runs npm install --legacy-peer-deps to regenerate the lockfile.

options.title: Pin webpack version explicitly
options.description: Adds webpack@5 to package.json resolutions field to force a single version and prevent the conflict.

options.title: Hold for manual resolution
options.description: Pause this incident and handle it manually.
